issue.title: Bug: PTY reader corrupts multi-byte UTF-8 across 4KB boundaries; also overloads IPC during Tab completion (E4)

phases.title: Refactor PTY reader thread: UTF-8 carryover + 10ms/64KB coalescing
verification: `cargo check --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` passes (no warnings introduced by the new code).
`cargo build --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` passes (Rust is locally verifiable; pnpm gates only the TS side).
Code review: the EOF branch flushes `leftover` (via lossy) AND `pending` before setting `should_break = true`. Without this, the terminal-exit event would race ahead of the final output.
Code review: the flush check runs unconditionally each loop iteration, not gated on the read result, so idle-after-burst still flushes within the 10 ms window.
Code review: the WouldBlock 10 ms sleep is gated on `is_would_block`; non-WouldBlock iterations loop immediately to drain large bursts.
Code review: `leftover.extend_from_slice(&combined[valid_up_to..])` only runs when `error_len() == None` (incomplete at end). Mid-stream errors take the lossy path and leftover remains empty.

Manual smoke (when the desktop app can be built): in a PowerShell terminal, run `for ($i = 0; $i -lt 2000; $i++) { Write-Host -NoNewline "🎉" }` — output contains exactly 2000 party-face emoji with zero U+FFFD replacement characters anywhere.
Manual smoke: `Get-ChildItem` in a directory with CJK filenames — names render correctly.
Manual smoke: in PowerShell, type `Get-` then press Tab — the cmdlet list appears without UI freeze; time-to-first-render under 100 ms.
Manual smoke: type a single character at the shell prompt — echo appears within one frame (no perceptible delay added by the 10 ms coalesce).
Manual smoke: run a short-lived command like `echo hi` — the output is visible BEFORE the terminal-exit indicator.
Manual smoke: long output stream like `Get-Content large.txt` — every line appears in scrollback (no bytes dropped to coalescing).
